title: Dorry Apologizes Before Falling
description: On pages 5–8, dorry apologizes before falling as the hidden Baroque Works agents expose their role in breaking the giants’ duel and begin turning the Straw Hats into targets.
scene: Dorry Apologizes Before Falling. The hidden Baroque Works agents expose their role in breaking the giants’ duel and begin turning the Straw Hats into targets. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: This is merely the judgment of the warrior God, Elbaf...!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Is that I am not blessed by his divine protection...!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: enough about God, divine protection, and whatever! That doesn't matter at all!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: If your god told you to die, would you willingly kill yourself!?

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Somebody else unfairly interferred with your duel!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: A duel that's been manipulated by a third party isn't a proper duel anymore!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: A little one like you who has only lived a mere 10 or 20 years,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Could not possibly comprehend Elbaf's Divine Words...!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Just get this off of me!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Gabababa! Dorry! After all these years of drinking only water, the rum must've tasted especially fine, right!?

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Just when I thought had met an amazing warrior...!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Why is he going so far for a giant he just met a minute ago...!? Is this really the way a wanted criminal acts...?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 3
dialogue.text: Just who is it that did such a lowly act...!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on dorry apologizes before falling. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
